---
title: "Posts Per User"
author: "James McQuilkin"
date: "2026-04-21"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#Load packages
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
library(scales)
```

```{r}
#Load and filter data
df <- read_csv(here("data/raw_data/twitter_data/twitter_na_2017-09_ny.csv.gz")) %>%
  filter(type == "ll")
```

```{r}
#Aggregate
posts_per_user <- df %>%
  count(u_id, name = "post_count")
```

```{r}
#Summary Statistics
summary_stats <- summary(posts_per_user$post_count)
print(summary_stats)
```

```{r}
#Visualization: Histogram with Log Scale
ggplot(posts_per_user, aes(x = post_count)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.1, fill = "blue", color = "white") + 
  scale_x_log10(breaks = trans_breaks("log10", function(x) 10^x),
                labels = trans_format("log10", math_format(10^.x))) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Posts per Account (Log Scale)",
    x = "Number of Posts (10^x)",
    y = "Count of Users"
  )
```

```{r}
#Visualization: Boxplot
ggplot(posts_per_user, aes(x = post_count)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "white", outlier.color = "red", outlier.shape = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Posts per Account - September 2017",
    x = "Number of Posts"
  )
```
